evals.prompt: I need to deprecate a function in my rOpenSci package and eventually remove it, without breaking my users. What's the recommended way to do this?
evals.expected_output: Two-stage deprecate -> defunct: first release mark .Deprecated('new') (warning, still runs) + pkgname-deprecated man page; next release .Defunct('new') (error) + add ... to absorb args + pkgname-defunct man page kept indefinitely; lifecycle package optional; don't change deprecated tests until defunct, then remove them; mentions argument/function rename patterns.

evals.prompt: I'm taking over maintenance of an rOpenSci package from someone who is stepping down. What do I need to update and be aware of?
evals.expected_output: In DESCRIPTION set new maintainer role=c('aut','cre') and make former maintainer 'aut' only; update maintainer name everywhere else (package man page, CONTRIBUTING.md, CITATION); CRAN: if active the old maintainer must email CRAN confirming change (mention in first submission); if archived/orphaned no permission needed; rOpenSci newsletter Call For Contributors; keep admin access / Slack.
